title: Data Protection & Privacy Policy
status: stub — chapter content to be populated
description: Northgate's data protection policy governing personal data processed as controller and processor: DPA terms, lawful basis, transfers, retention, breach handling and DPIAs. The most-cited policy in the contract corpus.

chapters.title: Purpose, Scope & Roles
chapters.synopsis: What the policy covers, definitions, and how Northgate's controller vs processor role is determined per engagement; interaction with the Contract Playbook and POL-SEC-011.

chapters.title: Lawful Basis & Marketing Consent
chapters.synopsis: Establishing lawful basis (Art. 6) for each processing purpose, including legitimate-interest assessments; consent standards for marketing lists (opted-in individuals only) and PECR requirements for electronic marketing.

chapters.title: Processor Terms & DPA Requirements
chapters.synopsis: Mandatory Art. 28(3) terms in every DPA — instructions, confidentiality, security, audit rights, deletion/return; substituting full processor terms where a supplier offers a thin DPA.

chapters.title: Sub-Processors
chapters.synopsis: Conditions for general written authorisation (Art. 28(2)), notification of new sub-processors, minimum objection window (30 days), and flow-down of processor obligations.

chapters.title: International Transfers & Safeguards
chapters.synopsis: Restricted-transfer rules and approved safeguards (Art. 46): EU SCCs (2021/914), UK IDTA/addendum, adequacy decisions; when to prefer EEA/UK-only hosting instead of transfer mechanisms.

chapters.title: Data Retention & Deletion
chapters.synopsis: Storage-limitation (Art. 5(1)(e)) and minimisation (Art. 5(1)(c)) requirements; retention schedules aligned to platform deletion capabilities (e.g. 30-day purge) and end-of-contract deletion/return commitments.

chapters.title: Special Category & High-Risk Data; DPIAs
chapters.synopsis: Handling of Art. 9 special-category data (health, etc.), scope exclusions to be confirmed in contract (no patient data / no student records), and when a DPIA (Art. 35) is required before signing an order form.

chapters.title: Personal Data Breach Notification
chapters.synopsis: Internal incident escalation and Art. 33 notification duties; contractual notification windows to customers (standard 48 hours — resist 12/24-hour demands) and coordination with POL-SEC-011 incident response.

chapters.title: Data Subject Rights & Audit Cooperation
chapters.synopsis: Assisting controllers with data-subject requests, audit and inspection rights under Art. 28(3)(h), and evidence/records to be maintained to demonstrate compliance.

chapters.title: Records of Processing & Accountability
chapters.synopsis: Maintaining the Art. 30 record of processing activities (controller and processor entries), the accountability principle (Art. 5(2)), and how processing records feed contract review and DPA negotiation.

chapters.title: Privacy Notices & Transparency
chapters.synopsis: Transparency obligations under Arts. 13–14: when privacy notices must be issued or updated, notice content standards, and layering for product, website and candidate/employee audiences.

chapters.title: Training & Awareness
chapters.synopsis: Mandatory data-protection training at induction and annual refresh, role-specific training for teams handling personal data (support, sales ops, engineering), and completion tracking.

chapters.title: Compliance, Exceptions & Breaches of this Policy
chapters.synopsis: Monitoring compliance with this policy, the process for requesting a documented exception (DPO approval required), and consequences of non-compliance including the disciplinary route for deliberate breaches.

chapters.title: Ownership, Review & Document Control
chapters.synopsis: Policy owner (DPO), annual review cycle and triggers for out-of-cycle review (new legislation, regulator guidance, incident learnings), version history and related documents (Contract Playbook, POL-SEC-011).
